lessons.rule: Ensure tsconfig.json includes "module": "ESNext" and "target": "ESNext" (or compatible) when using npx tsc with --module esnext --target esnext; otherwise compilation fails due to mismatched module/target settings.
Ensure all .tsx files have correct imports and that the tsconfig includes the proper paths; missing or incorrect imports cause TS syntax errors during tsc check.
Always validate TSX syntax before committing; ensure JSX tags are properly closed and file extensions are .tsx for components that contain JSX.
Always include a valid tsconfig.json with appropriate module and target settings (e.g., "module": "ESNext", "target": "ESNext", "moduleResolution": "node") before running tsc on mock test files.

Always verify that a valid tsconfig.json exists in the project root and that it includes the necessary compiler options (e.g., "module": "esnext", "target": "esnext", "esModuleInterop": true) before running tsc; otherwise the compile step will fail with syntax errors.

Always verify that TypeScript source files have correct syntax and that the tsconfig targets the appropriate module/target before running tsc; missing or incorrect import/export statements will cause compilation failures.

Always verify that TypeScript source files have correct syntax and that the tsconfig targets the appropriate module/esnext settings before running tsc; missing or malformed imports/type definitions will cause compilation failures.
Always validate TypeScript syntax before committing; run `tsc --noEmit` locally to catch syntax errors early.
Ensure TypeScript files have correct syntax (e.g., proper imports, semicolons, and module declarations) before running tsc; validate with `tsc --noEmit` to catch errors early.

When using `tsc` with `--skipLibCheck`, ensure that the project's `tsconfig.json` has appropriate `moduleResolution` and `types` settings (e.g., include `@types/node` for Node.js APIs) to avoid syntax errors during type checking.

Ensure the TypeScript compiler configuration (tsconfig.json) includes the correct module resolution and target settings for the project, and that all required type definitions are installed before running tsc checks.

Ensure all imported modules in TypeScript files have proper type definitions (install @types packages or declare custom .d.ts) before running tsc; otherwise the compiler will emit syntax errors on missing types.
